claims.summary: Telemetry connection and reconnect identities should remain durable.
claims.disposition: Verified WebSocket trace records connect attempts, active connection, valid payloads, and disconnects.

claims.summary: Database writes should not block the BridgeState write lock.
claims.disposition: Verified SQLite and trace persistence occurs after the state-lock scope.

claims.summary: Lock wait, lock hold, and prewrite work should be independently visible.
claims.disposition: Verified telemetry integration health and capture-only samples.

claims.summary: A high-frequency telemetry burst should test lock and pipeline latency.
claims.disposition: Routed to a bounded fake-server fixture with no live pressure induction.

claims.summary: A channel-based receiver refactor should be considered if measured lock or persistence thresholds are crossed.
claims.disposition: Routed to evidence-first architecture review; no pipeline change before a valid baseline.
